message:
chore(beads): sync issue tracker state

Update beads issue tracker with latest issue state changes from
concurrent development work.

Co-Authored-By: Claude <noreply@anthropic.com>

 {"id":"bd-1ndg","title":"Async I/O Traits Verification Suite (unit tests, adapters)","description":"# Async I/O Traits Verification Suite

## Purpose
Comprehensive verification for async I/O traits (imz, tokio-io equivalent) ensuring correct trait implementations and adapter behavior.

## Test Categories

### 1. Unit Tests
- AsyncRead: poll_read, read_buf
- AsyncWrite: poll_write, poll_flush, poll_shutdown
- AsyncBufRead: poll_fill_buf, consume
- AsyncSeek: poll_complete, start_seek
- Utility: read_to_end, read_to_string, read_exact
- Utility: write_all, copy, duplex

### 2. Adapter Tests
- BufReader: buffered reading
- BufWriter: buffered writing
- BufStream: bidirectional buffering
- Chain: sequential reading
- Take: limited reading
- Split: byte delimiter splitting
- Lines: line iterator
- ReadHalf/WriteHalf: split I/O

### 3. E2E Scenarios
| Scenario | Verifies |
|----------|----------|
| Copy stream | Efficient I/O copy |
| Buffered read chain | Adapter composition |
| Cancel during read_to_end | Partial result safety |
| Duplex echo | Bidirectional I/O |
| Split + join | Reader/writer split |

### 4. Trait Compliance Tests
- Implementors: TcpStream, File, etc.
- Contract verification: flush on drop
- Cancel-safety for all operations

## Logging Requirements
- I/O events logged with buffer sizes
- On failure: dump adapter state
- Byte throughput metrics

## Acceptance Criteria
- [ ] All traits have unit tests
- [ ] All adapters have unit tests
- [ ] E2E scenarios pass
- [ ] Trait compliance verified
- [ ] `cargo test io` passes deterministically

## Verification Commands
```bash
cargo test --package asupersync --lib io
cargo test --test io_e2e
```","status":"in_progress","priority":1,"issue_type":"feature","assignee":"BrownValley","created_at":"2026-01-22T19:49:07.382659203Z","created_by":"ubuntu","updated_at":"2026-01-23T07:07:43.123357153Z","compaction_level":0,"original_size":0,"dependencies":[{"issue_id":"bd-1ndg","depends_on_id":"asupersync-imz","type":"blocks","created_at":"2026-01-27T06:20:45Z","created_by":"import"}]}
 {"id":"bd-1q6r","title":"[BLOCKER] Fix transport/router.rs compilation errors","description":"The main library does not compile. cargo check --lib fails with 9 errors in src/transport/router.rs.

Key issues:
1. dyn SymbolSink doesn't implement Debug (line 843)
2. Uses self.table instead of self.router.table() (lines 997-999, 1043)
3. Returns RoutingError when function expects DispatchError (lines 1000-1003, 1014, 1058)

Impact: All integration tests blocked, no agents can verify work.

Fix: Manual Debug impl, correct field access, wrap errors in DispatchError::RoutingFailed.","status":"closed","priority":0,"issue_type":"bug","created_at":"2026-01-21T07:21:47.274825603Z","created_by":"ubuntu","updated_at":"2026-01-21T08:20:51.948345676Z","closed_at":"2026-01-21T08:20:51.948183230Z","close_reason":"Compilation errors fixed - cargo check passes with only warnings","compaction_level":0,"original_size":0}
 {"id":"bd-1rn8","title":"Async Streams Verification Suite (unit tests, E2E, combinators)","description":"# Async Streams Verification Suite

## Purpose
Comprehensive verification for async streams (x3p, tokio-stream equivalent) ensuring combinator correctness and cancel-safety.

## Test Categories

### 1. Unit Tests
- Stream trait: poll_next
- StreamExt: map, filter, filter_map, take, skip
- StreamExt: chain, zip, merge, select
- StreamExt: fold, collect, for_each
- Channel streams: mpsc, broadcast, watch
- Interval stream: tick, tick_with

### 2. E2E Scenarios
| Scenario | Verifies |
|----------|----------|
| Basic iteration | Stream consumption |
| Map + filter chain | Combinator composition |
| Merge streams | Interleaved output |
| Backpressure | Slow consumer handling |
| Cancel mid-stream | Clean termination |
| Infinite stream + take | Bounded consumption |
| Error propagation | Stream error handling |

### 3. Cancel-Safety Tests
- Cancel during next(): no item loss
- Cancel during collect(): partial results
- Cancel merged stream: all sources cancelled

### 4. Memory Tests
- No buffering leaks
- Bounded memory for infinite streams
- Drop semantics for pending items

## Logging Requirements
- Stream events logged with stream IDs
- On failure: dump pending items, combinator state
- Item flow metrics: produced, consumed, dropped

## Acceptance Criteria
- [ ] All combinators have unit tests
- [ ] E2E scenarios pass under lab runtime
- [ ] Cancel-safety verified for all operations
- [ ] Memory bounded for all scenarios
- [ ] `cargo test stream` passes deterministically

## Verification Commands
```bash
cargo test --package asupersync --lib stream
cargo test --test stream_e2e
```","status":"in_progress","priority":1,"issue_type":"feature","assignee":"ClaudeOpus4.5-Session","created_at":"2026-01-22T19:48:15.585218049Z","created_by":"ubuntu","updated_at":"2026-01-23T05:22:50.755907875Z","compaction_level":0,"original_size":0,"dependencies":[{"issue_id":"bd-1rn8","depends_on_id":"asupersync-x3p","type":"blocks","created_at":"2026-01-27T06:20:45Z","created_by":"import"}]}

Remaining errors are in test files which use the old synchronous channel API instead of the new async API. Tests need to be updated to use try_send() instead of send().await. Fixed files: src/runtime/reactor/source.rs, src/runtime/reactor/registration.rs, src/cx/cx.rs, src/net/unix/listener.rs, src/net/unix/stream.rs, src/net/unix/split.rs, src/stream/forward.rs, src/stream/broadcast_stream.rs, src/stream/receiver_stream.rs, src/stream/mod.rs. Creating follow-up bead for test fixes.","created_at":"2026-01-28T18:49:27Z"}]}
 {"id":"bd-221m","title":"Service Layer Verification Suite (unit tests, E2E, middleware)","description":"# Service Layer Verification Suite

## Purpose
Comprehensive verification for the service layer (lnm, tower equivalent) ensuring composability, cancel-correctness, and middleware stack behavior.

## Test Categories

### 1. Unit Tests
- Service trait: call, poll_ready
- Layer trait: composition
- ServiceBuilder: layer chaining
- Standard middleware: timeout, rate limit, retry, concurrency limit

### 2. E2E Scenarios
| Scenario | Verifies |
|----------|----------|
| Basic service call | Request/response lifecycle |
| Timeout middleware | Budget integration, timeout enforcement |
| Rate limiting | Request throttling, backpressure |
| Retry with backoff | Exponential backoff, max attempts |
| Circuit breaker | Open/half-open/closed states |
| Bulkhead isolation | Resource partitioning |
| Middleware stack | Correct layering order |

### 3. Cancel-Safety Tests
- Cancel mid-request: clean abort
- Cancel with pending retries: no orphan requests
- Cancel with rate limit queue: queue cleanup

### 4. Load Tests
- Concurrent requests
- Backpressure behavior
- Resource exhaustion handling

## Logging Requirements
- Middleware events logged with request IDs
- On failure: dump middleware stack state
- Request/response timings logged

## Acceptance Criteria
- [ ] All middleware have unit tests
- [ ] E2E scenarios pass with real HTTP backend
- [ ] Cancel-safety verified for all middleware
- [ ] Load tests pass without resource leaks
- [ ] `cargo test service` passes deterministically

## Verification Commands
```bash
cargo test --package asupersync --lib service
cargo test --test service_e2e
```","status":"open","priority":1,"issue_type":"feature","created_at":"2026-01-22T19:47:11.707559049Z","created_by":"ubuntu","updated_at":"2026-01-22T19:47:33.134533963Z","compaction_level":0,"original_size":0,"dependencies":[{"issue_id":"bd-221m","depends_on_id":"asupersync-lnm","type":"blocks","created_at":"2026-01-27T06:20:45Z","created_by":"import"}]}
 {"id":"bd-22vb","title":"Codec Framework Verification Suite (unit tests, E2E, framing)","description":"# Codec Framework Verification Suite

## Purpose
Comprehensive verification for the codec framework (4nz, tokio-util codecs equivalent) ensuring correct encoding/decoding and framing.

## Test Categories

### 1. Unit Tests
- Decoder trait: decode, decode_eof
- Encoder trait: encode
- FramedRead: framed decoding
- FramedWrite: framed encoding
- Framed: bidirectional codec
- Common codecs: LinesCodec, BytesCodec, LengthDelimitedCodec

### 2. Codec Implementation Tests
- Lines: newline delimiter
- Bytes: raw passthrough
- LengthDelimited: length-prefixed frames
- JSON: serde_json codec
- Custom codec patterns

### 3. E2E Scenarios
| Scenario | Verifies |
|----------|----------|
| Line-delimited protocol | Text protocol parsing |
| Length-prefixed frames | Binary protocol framing |
| Partial frame handling | Buffer management |
| Large frame handling | Memory efficiency |
| Cancel mid-frame | Clean abort |
| Codec chaining | Nested framing |
| Error propagation | Decode error handling |

### 4. Edge Cases
- Empty frames
- Maximum frame size
- Incomplete frames at EOF
- Malformed input
- Concurrent encode/decode

### 5. Performance Tests
- Throughput for various codecs
- Memory allocation per frame
- Zero-copy where possible

## Logging Requirements
- Codec events logged with frame sizes
- On failure: dump buffer state, codec state
- Frame metrics: encoded, decoded, errors

## Acceptance Criteria
- [ ] All codecs have unit tests
- [ ] E2E scenarios pass
- [ ] Edge cases handled correctly
- [ ] Performance baselines established
- [ ] `cargo test codec` passes deterministically

## Verification Commands
```bash
cargo test --package asupersync --lib codec
cargo test --test codec_e2e
```","status":"in_progress","priority":1,"issue_type":"feature","assignee":"HazyRidge","created_at":"2026-01-22T19:49:24.986532463Z","created_by":"ubuntu","updated_at":"2026-01-23T07:07:56.254159284Z","compaction_level":0,"original_size":0,"dependencies":[{"issue_id":"bd-22vb","depends_on_id":"asupersync-4nz","type":"blocks","created_at":"2026-01-27T06:20:45Z","created_by":"import"}]}
 {"id":"bd-2520","title":"Pool integration tests incorrectly use try_acquire expecting resource creation","description":"Tests in tests/pool_tests.rs call try_acquire() expecting it to create new resources, but try_acquire() only returns idle resources. Tests should use acquire() (async) or pre-populate the pool. Affects 11 failing tests.","status":"closed","priority":2,"issue_type":"bug","assignee":"IvoryMoose","created_at":"2026-01-26T09:01:31.029002779Z","created_by":"ubuntu","updated_at":"2026-01-26T09:04:41.620395437Z","closed_at":"2026-01-26T09:04:41.620326478Z","compaction_level":0,"original_size":0}

 {"id":"bd-3p6e","title":"[Harmonize] Migrate orphan sub-tasks to hierarchical IDs","description":"# [Harmonize] Migrate orphan sub-tasks to hierarchical IDs

## Purpose
Ensure all sub-tasks use hierarchical IDs following the {epic-id}.{n}.{m} pattern.

## Orphans to Migrate
- asupersync-8z9 → under n5o or xrc
- asupersync-ior → under n5o or xrc
- asupersync-9d3 → under n5o or xrc
- asupersync-c61 → under n5o or xrc

## Hierarchical ID Pattern
- EPIC: asupersync-{id}
- Sub-EPIC: asupersync-{epic}.{n}
- Task: asupersync-{epic}.{n}.{m}

## Acceptance Criteria
- [ ] All orphan sub-tasks linked to parent EPICs
- [ ] New beads use hierarchical ID pattern
- [ ] `br show {epic}` shows complete child tree
- [ ] No floating sub-tasks without parent dependencies

## Verification
```bash
bv --robot-suggest | jq '.suggestions | map(select(.type == "orphan"))'
```","status":"closed","priority":1,"issue_type":"task","created_at":"2026-01-22T18:28:34.679412816Z","created_by":"ubuntu","updated_at":"2026-01-23T02:31:36.812205071Z","closed_at":"2026-01-23T02:31:36.812110173Z","close_reason":"Linked orphan tasks to asupersync-xrc as parent-child (8z9/ior/9d3/c61); verified no cycles","compaction_level":0,"original_size":0,"dependencies":[{"issue_id":"bd-3p6e","depends_on_id":"bd-zs64","type":"parent-child","created_at":"2026-01-27T06:20:45Z","created_by":"import"}],"comments":[{"id":10,"issue_id":"bd-3p6e","author":"Dicklesworthstone","text":"Added parent-child deps to asupersync-xrc for legacy tasks: asupersync-8z9, asupersync-ior, asupersync-9d3, asupersync-c61. Verified no cycles.","created_at":"2026-01-23T02:31:29Z"}]}
 {"id":"bd-3qc6","title":"Cancel-Aware Sync Primitives Verification Suite (unit tests, E2E)","description":"# Cancel-Aware Sync Primitives Verification Suite

## Purpose
Comprehensive verification for cancel-aware synchronization primitives (q48) ensuring correctness under cancellation and two-phase permit semantics.

## Test Categories

### 1. Unit Tests
- Mutex: lock, try_lock, cancel-during-wait
- RwLock: read, write, upgrade, downgrade
- Semaphore: acquire, try_acquire, two-phase permits
- Barrier: wait, reset
- Notify: notify_one, notify_all, notify_waiters
- OnceCell: get_or_init, get_or_try_init

### 2. E2E Scenarios
| Scenario | Verifies |
|----------|----------|
| Mutex contention | Fair queuing, no starvation |
| RwLock readers | Concurrent read access |
| RwLock writer priority | Writer preference |
| Semaphore permits | Count accuracy |
| Semaphore two-phase | Reserve/commit/abort |
| Barrier sync | All tasks reach barrier |
| Notify wake ordering | FIFO wake semantics |
| OnceCell race | Single initialization |

### 3. Cancel-Safety Tests
- Cancel while waiting for mutex: no leak
- Cancel while holding permit: proper release
- Cancel during barrier wait: barrier reset
- Cancel during semaphore reserve: abort semantics

### 4. Stress Tests
- High contention scenarios
- Many waiters, few resources
- Rapid acquire/release cycles
- Mixed read/write patterns

### 5. Deadlock Detection Tests
- Cycle detection in lock graphs
- Priority inversion scenarios
- Lab runtime deadlock oracle

## Logging Requirements
- Lock events logged with task IDs
- On failure: dump waiter queues, lock state
- Contention metrics: wait times, queue lengths

## Acceptance Criteria
- [ ] All primitives have unit tests
- [ ] Cancel-safety verified for all operations
- [ ] No deadlocks in stress tests
- [ ] Two-phase permits work correctly
- [ ] `cargo test sync` passes deterministically

## Verification Commands
```bash
cargo test --package asupersync --lib sync
cargo test --test sync_e2e
```","status":"in_progress","priority":1,"issue_type":"feature","assignee":"MagentaReef","created_at":"2026-01-22T19:47:57.306837602Z","created_by":"ubuntu","updated_at":"2026-01-23T03:34:21.891711941Z","compaction_level":0,"original_size":0,"dependencies":[{"issue_id":"bd-3qc6","depends_on_id":"asupersync-q48","type":"blocks","created_at":"2026-01-27T06:20:45Z","created_by":"import"}]}
 {"id":"bd-3rpb","title":"Fix unsafe impl Send in pool.rs","status":"closed","priority":1,"issue_type":"bug","created_at":"2026-01-21T06:45:09.216873525Z","created_by":"ubuntu","updated_at":"2026-01-21T06:45:22.983852136Z","closed_at":"2026-01-21T06:45:22.983801701Z","close_reason":"Fixed","compaction_level":0,"original_size":0}
+{"id":"bd-3sfb","title":"Fix test files using old synchronous channel API","description":"Tests need to be updated to use try_send() instead of the async send().await. Affected test files include net_unix, phase0_verification, stream_e2e, and inline tests in channel/mpsc.rs, stream/mod.rs, etc. The channel API changed from sync to async so tests that call send() synchronously need to use try_send() instead.","status":"in_progress","priority":1,"issue_type":"task","assignee":"GoldGate","created_at":"2026-01-28T18:49:42.627917220Z","created_by":"ubuntu","updated_at":"2026-01-28T18:51:01.893317365Z","compaction_level":0,"original_size":0}

Tests: verify_empty_trace, verify_valid_trace, quick_verification, corrupted_event_detection, detect_timeline_non_monotonic, detect_truncated_file, partial_recovery_info, compat_reader_reads_valid_trace, compat_reader_skips_unknown_events, disk_full_is_handled","notes":"Fixed - compression byte was missing in verify_header and CompatReader::open","status":"closed","priority":2,"issue_type":"bug","created_at":"2026-01-26T16:28:11.226980182Z","created_by":"ubuntu","updated_at":"2026-01-26T16:37:39.714703099Z","closed_at":"2026-01-26T16:37:39.714633969Z","compaction_level":0,"original_size":0}
 {"id":"bd-4rpn","title":"[Harmonize] Fix priority alignments","description":"# [Harmonize] Fix priority alignments

## Purpose
Ensure priority follows dependency order: parent EPICs have priority ≤ child priorities.

## Issues Fixed
- xrc (Phase 1) was P2, now P1 (blocks ds8 which is P2)
- 90l9 (P0 duplicate) closed/merged into ds8

## Rule
Priority must respect dependencies:
- If A blocks B, then priority(A) ≤ priority(B)
- P0 = Critical, P1 = High, P2 = Medium, P3 = Low, P4 = Backlog

## Acceptance Criteria
- [ ] No priority inversions in dependency graph
- [ ] `bv --robot-priority` shows no misalignments
- [ ] All xrc.* sub-tasks have P1 priority
- [ ] Parent EPICs never have higher priority number than children

## Verification
```bash
bv --robot-priority | jq '.recommendations'  # Should be empty or acceptable
```","status":"closed","priority":0,"issue_type":"task","assignee":"ClaudeOpus45","created_at":"2026-01-22T18:28:29.875907033Z","created_by":"ubuntu","updated_at":"2026-01-23T03:14:34.794684288Z","closed_at":"2026-01-23T02:35:47.483747422Z","close_reason":"Fixed priority inversions:
- 14o: P0→P1 (match P1 dependencies)
- w9rc: P0→P1 (blocked by 14o)
- 4qw: P2→P1 (match P1 children)
- 4ul: P1→P2 (blocked by ds8)
- 6ll,9mq: P1→P3 (blocked by P3 items)
- y1p,0vx,ucq,zfn: P1→P2 (cascading fixes)
- tpru: P2→P3, brm: P2→P3, ou7o: P2→P3, 3nm: P2→P3
- z8n,5jm,m76: P1→P2 (blocked by 4ul)
No cycles detected. Core xrc.* tasks are P1, stretch goals (xrc.5,xrc.6) appropriately P3/P4.","compaction_level":0,"original_size":0,"dependencies":[{"issue_id":"bd-4rpn","depends_on_id":"bd-zs64","type":"parent-child","created_at":"2026-01-27T06:20:45Z","created_by":"import"}],"comments":[{"id":11,"issue_id":"bd-4rpn","author":"Dicklesworthstone","text":"Ran a priority inversion scan against .beads/issues.jsonl: 42 inversions where a blocker has lower priority (numerically higher). Notables: asupersync-xrc P1 blocks bd-hzrb P0 (also asupersync-n5o P1 blocks bd-hzrb P0), asupersync-akx.2.2 P1 blocks asupersync-akx.2 P0, asupersync-jdg P1 blocks asupersync-akx.7 P0, asupersync-0a0 P2 blocks multiple P1 tasks (4v1/573/9r7/h10/uls/xtx), asupersync-r2n P2 blocks 4v1/9r7/fxd P1. Full list available on request.","created_at":"2026-01-23T03:14:34Z"}]}
